title: The Cyber House Rules

jokes.text: They were delicious. But, I digress. Tremble, puny earthlings! One day my race will destroy you all!
jokes.setup: Character starts with a normal compliment about cookies
jokes.punchline: Suddenly shifts to threatening world domination
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect comedic timing. The abrupt shift from mundane to megalomaniacal is well-crafted.

jokes.text: I was abandoned as a baby too. So... - Garbage, huh? I'll take care of it.
jokes.setup: Leela tries to bond over shared experience of abandonment
jokes.punchline: Bender completely misses the emotional moment and treats the baby as literal garbage
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - Bender's callous robot nature contrasts beautifully with Leela's empathy.

jokes.text: Bender, stop! It's a baby! - A baby what?
jokes.setup: Standard clarification that it's a human baby
jokes.punchline: Bender's literal robot logic questions what species of baby
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender logic - treating 'baby' as an incomplete descriptor rather than understanding the emotional context.

jokes.text: Please stand clear of self-destructing basket.
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic self-destruction warning for a simple basket
jokes.explanation: Typical Futurama future-tech absurdity - unnecessarily dramatic technology for mundane tasks.

jokes.text: No way, Jose-bot.
jokes.punchline: Robot-themed variation 'Jose-bot'
jokes.explanation: Simple robot-themed wordplay. Functional but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: How can you make fun of me? You're blind. - My eyes may not work, but at least I got two of them.
jokes.setup: Leela points out the irony of a blind kid mocking her eye
jokes.punchline: Blind kid doubles down with twisted logic about quantity over quality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - the blind kid's logic is both cruel and absurdly reasonable within its own twisted framework.

jokes.text: Imagine the look on their faces when we eat all the hors d'oeuvres.
jokes.setup: Leela considers showing off her success
jokes.punchline: Bender's petty revenge fantasy involves consuming all the fancy food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - turning emotional revenge into simple gluttony. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Gosh, the bars on the windows seemed so much thicker back then.
jokes.punchline: Reveals the orphanarium was essentially a prison
jokes.explanation: Dark twist on childhood nostalgia - recontextualizing innocent memories as dystopian reality.

jokes.text: Leela, you're worthless, and no one will ever love you! - You used to say that all the time. - Oh, those were happier days.
jokes.punchline: Both characters fondly remember the psychological abuse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - the mutual nostalgia for abuse is both shocking and perfectly structured. The escalation from insult to fond memory is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: The gristle in a blanket isn't half bad. - Try the Popsicle sticks. They've absorbed a lot of flavor.
jokes.punchline: Even worse suggestion - eating flavored wooden sticks
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of terrible food joke. The popsicle stick line is appropriately gross and absurd.

jokes.text: I don't see you anywhere. - That's me over in Cootietown.
jokes.setup: Looking for Leela in old group photo
jokes.punchline: She was segregated in the social outcast section
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline structure revealing the extent of Leela's childhood ostracism through concrete visual detail.

jokes.text: One-eye! One-eye! - Stupid as a French guy!
jokes.setup: Standard schoolyard taunting about Leela's eye
jokes.punchline: Completely random and illogical French insult
jokes.explanation: Random = funny approach that somewhat works due to the complete non-sequitur nature, but lacks craft.

jokes.text: Living in a box. You? - Selling kidneys, teeth, whatever.
jokes.punchline: Reveals horrifically desperate adult lives through casual tone
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect casual tone. The escalation from homelessness to organ selling is grimly funny.

jokes.text: And what am I up to, you ask? Why, I'm a very successful space captain.
jokes.setup: Leela tries to impress with her career
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic delivery reveals her insecurity and need to compensate
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Leela's defensive pride, but the joke itself is fairly standard.

jokes.text: That's so good for a person with one eye. - Hey, you can't feel sorry for me. I'm a space captain, and you're a bunch of losers. - Right, right. We're the losers.
jokes.punchline: Orphans sarcastically agree they're the losers while clearly living better lives
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Leela's attempt to assert superiority backfires as the 'losers' clearly pity her.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? Well, shut up, cane-boy! - He can't hear you. He's deaf now.
jokes.setup: Leela tries to insult the blind kid with cane reference
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's now also deaf, making the insult completely ineffective
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of disability humor - Leela's weak comeback becomes completely pointless due to added handicap.

jokes.text: She's not gussied up, duded out or where it's at.
jokes.punchline: Uses hilariously outdated slang that makes him sound incredibly uncool
jokes.explanation: Great character establishment - Adlai's attempt to be hip reveals how unhip he is. Good period slang usage.

jokes.text: I guess you never outgrow being an eyeball. Oddball.
jokes.punchline: Accidentally says 'eyeball' first, revealing her self-consciousness
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - the slip reveals Leela's deep insecurity about her eye. Perfect wordplay that feels natural and earned.

jokes.text: A package is just a box until it's delivered.
jokes.punchline: Adlai's attempt at profound wisdom is completely meaningless
jokes.explanation: Perfect pseudo-philosophical nonsense that sounds wise but is meaningless. Great character establishment for Adlai.

jokes.text: A tall doctor, you say?
jokes.punchline: Leela mishears it as 'tall doctor' and focuses on wrong detail
jokes.explanation: Standard mishearing joke. Functional but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: It's a great way to have a kid without sex. - Really?
jokes.punchline: Man is genuinely excited about avoiding sex rather than gaining a child
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline revealing the man's twisted priorities and Bender's sales technique.

jokes.text: A hundred dollars a week?
jokes.punchline: Bender's greedy excitement is palpable in his repetition
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender greed moment. Good character integration but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: I broke that blind kid's nose, and it made no difference.
jokes.punchline: Reveals past violence against disabled child with casual delivery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - the casual revelation of violence against a disabled child is shocking yet perfectly delivered. The futility adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: It won't be able to see, but you'll look like a normal two-eyed person.
jokes.punchline: The 'corrective' surgery only addresses appearance, not function
jokes.explanation: Good satire of cosmetic surgery culture - prioritizing appearance over functionality reveals shallow values.

jokes.text: Why are they with you? Do you have candy? - No. It's called parenting.
jokes.setup: Fry's confusion about Bender having children
jokes.punchline: Bender's matter-of-fact explanation of a concept foreign to both
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing both characters' unfamiliarity with normal family dynamics.

jokes.text: Our daddy's a giant toy!
jokes.punchline: Innocent child's perspective on robot parent
jokes.explanation: Sweet child's logic about their robot father. Harmless but not particularly strong comedy.

jokes.text: I used to be too cute, so I had cuteness reduction surgery here and here.
jokes.punchline: Amy's fictional backstory about being too attractive
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion of cosmetic surgery tropes. Amy's vanity expressed through fictional past cuteness is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You're better, you're abnormal!
jokes.punchline: His attempt at a compliment becomes an insult through word choice
jokes.explanation: Typical Fry verbal fumble - his heart's in the right place but his words aren't. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I'll start by not caring what you think.
jokes.setup: Fry says she shouldn't care what people think
jokes.punchline: Leela applies his advice directly to him
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback with good logical structure. Leela turns Fry's advice against him perfectly.

jokes.text: You can also get an ink pouch to help you escape your enemies.
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg suggests cuttlefish-like modifications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - applying his crustacean biology to human situations. Very character-specific humor.

jokes.text: That's the stupidest idea I've ever heard, you imbecile!
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg angrily rejects idea that would benefit his own species
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Zoidberg's self-loathing extends to rejecting his own species' advantages.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. This is the wrong patient.
jokes.punchline: Classic medical mix-up undermining the dramatic moment
jokes.explanation: Standard medical comedy trope. Functions as misdirection but lacks originality.

jokes.text: She looked fine before. - No. - Wrong.
jokes.setup: Fry's opinion about Leela's previous appearance
jokes.punchline: Immediate, blunt contradictions from the crew
jokes.explanation: Good timing and economy but fairly predictable gang-up on Fry's minority opinion.

jokes.text: I try not to blink too much, because it's flashy. But when I do, I enjoy it.
jokes.setup: Leela learning to use two eyes
jokes.punchline: Overthinking the simple act of blinking with absurd reasoning
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Leela's self-consciousness about normal functions. The logic is endearingly absurd.

jokes.text: I did it! I blunk!
jokes.punchline: Made-up past tense 'blunk' shows her excitement and linguistic creativity
jokes.explanation: Charming made-up word that perfectly captures Leela's excitement. Highly quotable and endearing.

jokes.text: Kids have names?
jokes.punchline: Bender's genuine surprise that children are individuals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender parenting - treating children as interchangeable units rather than individuals.

jokes.text: Now you're all named Bender Jr.!
jokes.setup: Children introducing themselves with individual names
jokes.punchline: Bender eliminates their individuality with mass renaming
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Bender's parenting style - complete erasure of children's identities for his convenience.

jokes.text: Twelve baby humans, 1200 wing-wangs.
jokes.punchline: Reduces children to mathematical formula with funny currency
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender greed with future currency. Good character consistency but predictable.

jokes.text: What is it with you kids? Every other day, it's food, food, food!
jokes.punchline: Bender treats basic survival needs as unreasonable demands
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of bad parenting - treating children's basic needs as inconveniences. Very quotable.

jokes.text: The cat shelter's onto me.
jokes.punchline: Implies he's been stealing cats to make hamburgers
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually. The implication of cat-burgers is both horrible and perfectly Bender.

jokes.text: I was getting one of my shirts toned down, so I dropped in for your exam.
jokes.setup: Adlai explaining his presence at Leela's appointment
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions getting shirts 'toned down' as if it's normal
jokes.explanation: Great character detail - Adlai's blandness extends to having his clothes made more boring. Perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Perfect. A guy could fall head over heels for you. - I'm interested myself.
jokes.punchline: Adlai's awkward, clinical way of expressing romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Adlai's emotionally stilted personality. His romantic interest sounds like a medical diagnosis.

jokes.text: I'm not good with words, so I'm in a delicate conundrum.
jokes.setup: Adlai claims he's not good with words
jokes.punchline: Immediately uses sophisticated vocabulary like 'delicate conundrum'
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic contradiction - claims verbal inadequacy while demonstrating pretentious vocabulary. Great character work.

jokes.text: Most doctors are rich. - When did this happen? - You're joking, right? That's not funny!
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's increasing panic about missing out on doctor wealth
jokes.explanation: Great Zoidberg moment - his desperation about being a poor doctor escalates beautifully from confusion to panic.

jokes.text: Let me ditch the kids in an alley, and we can go have some fun.
jokes.setup: Bender trying to impress a woman
jokes.punchline: Casually suggests abandoning children for a date
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about child abandonment delivered with perfect Bender callousness. Shocking but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Daddy's trying to score with a cheap floozy right now, so cram a Tinkertoy in it.
jokes.punchline: Bender explains adult situation inappropriately to child with toy reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy - inappropriate adult language mixed with childish toy reference. Perfect horrible parenting moment.

jokes.text: I love you, Daddy Bender. - What the hell? Quit hugging me!
jokes.punchline: Bender's complete rejection of affection
jokes.explanation: Touching moment turned comedic by Bender's inability to handle genuine emotion. Good emotional contrast.

jokes.text: I've got half a mind to hug each and every one of you, and see how you like it!
jokes.setup: Bender threatening punishment for the kids
jokes.punchline: Threatens to show affection as if it's torture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion - threatening love as punishment perfectly captures Bender's emotional dysfunction. Highly quotable and character-perfect.

jokes.text: I'd like an extra-beautiful bouquet for my extra-gorgeous sweetheart. - Average, please.
jokes.punchline: Leela's request for the most mundane option possible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Leela's newfound obsession with normalcy. Perfect economy and great subversion of romantic expectations.

jokes.text: You're 999,999 in a million.
jokes.setup: Adlai trying to give Leela a romantic compliment
jokes.punchline: Mathematical error makes the compliment technically an insult
jokes.explanation: Clever mathematical joke showing Adlai's attempt at romance backfiring through poor math. Perfectly character-appropriate awkwardness.

jokes.text: He's a weird monster who eats garbage. - Damn right.
jokes.setup: Fry describing Zoidberg as part of their weird crew
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg proudly embraces the insulting description
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Zoidberg's self-acceptance and pride in being weird. Classic Zoidberg response.

jokes.text: Tally me banana.
jokes.setup: Being described as a Rastafarian accountant
jokes.punchline: Hermes responds with Jamaican-themed accounting pun
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural wordplay combining Jamaican song reference with accounting. Perfect for Hermes's character.

jokes.text: Hermes is a Rastafarian accountant. - Tally me banana.
jokes.punchline: Hermes' Jamaican-inflected accounting reference
jokes.explanation: Cute wordplay combining 'Tally me banana' with accounting theme. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I already did!
jokes.setup: Amy mentioning Fry has 'that brain thing'
jokes.punchline: Fry's defensive reaction implies he's aware of his stupidity
jokes.explanation: Standard Fry self-awareness moment. Good character consistency but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: What do we say when you get something? - About time!
jokes.punchline: Child gives rude response instead of 'thank you'
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expected politeness lesson. Shows Bender is teaching the wrong values.

jokes.text: I gotta go poopy! - You should've gone before it was time to run.
jokes.punchline: Bender treating children like criminal accomplices in a heist
jokes.explanation: Reveals Bender is using children for dine-and-dash schemes. His criminal expectations of toddlers is absurd.

jokes.text: You little crooks! If I catch you, I'm gonna make cutie-muffin gumbo!
jokes.setup: Kids running away from restaurant without paying
jokes.punchline: Threatens cannibalism but with cutesy terminology
jokes.explanation: Dark humor mixing violence with cute language. The contrast between 'cutie-muffin' and cannibalism is effectively jarring.

jokes.text: It's time to stop living this vida bachelor loca and have kids.
jokes.punchline: Mixes Spanish and English awkwardly in attempt to sound hip
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Adlai's attempts to be cool falling flat. The awkward language mixing is perfectly him.

jokes.text: Bender's Arrest Record, by the police.
jokes.setup: Kids asking for a bedtime story
jokes.punchline: Bender reads his actual criminal record as children's literature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of bedtime story tropes. Using police records as children's entertainment is perfectly horrible Bender parenting.

jokes.text: I get 100 bucks a kid, and they're costing 110!
jokes.punchline: Discovers he's losing money on each child
jokes.explanation: Classic business comedy - the greedy scheme backfiring through basic economics. Good Bender character moment.

jokes.text: Imperial Dragon restaurant? I've got a herd of you-know-whats for sale.
jokes.punchline: Heavily implies he's selling children to a Chinese restaurant
jokes.explanation: Extremely dark humor about child trafficking delivered casually. The euphemistic language makes it even more disturbing and funny.

jokes.text: Imperial Dragon restaurant? I've got a herd of you-know-whats for sale.
jokes.punchline: Implication he's selling children to restaurant as food ingredients
jokes.explanation: Extremely dark humor escalating Bender's terrible parenting to child trafficking. The euphemism makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Bender's Orphanarium means discount orphans.
jokes.punchline: Treats children like discounted merchandise
jokes.explanation: Good business parody - treating adoption like a used car lot. Classic Bender commodification of everything.

jokes.text: Kind of fatty.
jokes.setup: Albert introducing himself to potential parents
jokes.punchline: Adlai's blunt, inappropriate assessment of child
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Adlai's tactlessness. The blunt delivery makes it effectively uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Purebred human. No vampire in there.
jokes.setup: Bender describing a child for adoption
jokes.punchline: Treats humans like dog breeds with vampire concerns
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor treating human adoption like pet breeding. The vampire disclaimer is perfectly random.

jokes.text: Cursed with a third ear but full of an emotion I understand is called love.
jokes.setup: Bender describing Sally to potential parents
jokes.punchline: Clinical description of love as foreign emotion he doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender emotional disconnect - treating love as an alien concept while trying to sell a child. Excellent character work.

jokes.text: You princes of Maine, you kings of New New England.
jokes.setup: Bender saying goodnight to the children
jokes.punchline: References 'The Cider House Rules' in surprisingly tender moment
jokes.explanation: Literary reference showing unexpected depth from Bender. The genuine tenderness is surprising and touching.

jokes.text: If you had kids of your own, you'd understand!
jokes.setup: Bender being arrested for child abuse
jokes.punchline: Defends his parenting while being arrested for terrible parenting
jokes.explanation: Good ironic defense - claiming parental understanding while demonstrating complete lack thereof. Classic Bender obliviousness.

jokes.text: Ear-face, Ear-face Sally won the ugly race
jokes.setup: Children bullying Sally about her deformity
jokes.punchline: Cruel rhyming chant about child's appearance
jokes.explanation: Standard schoolyard cruelty. Serves story purpose but isn't particularly clever comedy writing.

jokes.text: She doesn't need an operation. She's fine this way. - And you were fine the way you were? - Damn right I was!
jokes.punchline: Realizing the hypocrisy of her own surgical choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment where Leela realizes her mistake through defending another. Great emotional and comedic payoff.

jokes.text: I also have a tail.
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals additional abnormality
jokes.explanation: Good escalation delivered with perfect casualness. The deadpan delivery of increasingly weird details works well.

jokes.text: By the end of the day, one of us is gonna have one eye.
jokes.setup: Leela threatening Adlai to reverse her surgery
jokes.punchline: Implies she'll remove one of his eyes if he doesn't comply
jokes.explanation: Excellent threat with perfect mathematical logic. The implication of eye removal is both menacing and absurdly logical.

jokes.text: We know robots don't have emotions, but we drawed you this picture.
jokes.punchline: Acknowledge his emotionlessness while showing genuine affection
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment with good irony - the kids understand Bender's nature but love him anyway. The grammar 'drawed' adds authenticity.

jokes.text: Hey, I smoke a cigar, not a candy cane.
jokes.setup: Kids showing their drawing of Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender critiques their artistic accuracy rather than appreciating the gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - missing the emotional point entirely to focus on technical details. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Hey, I smoke a cigar, not a candy cane.
jokes.punchline: Bender correcting artistic inaccuracies in child's loving gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - focusing on technical details while missing emotional significance. Character-consistent deflection.

jokes.text: I hate you! I hate you all!
jokes.punchline: Bender's emotional denial while being overwhelmed by affection
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender emotional denial. His protests against love while being physically overwhelmed by hugs is perfectly in character.

tmdb_overview: Leela meets up with her former orphanarium playmate Adlai Atkins, now a plastic surgeon, who offers to grant Leela surgery that will give her two eyes. Meanwhile, Bender adopts twelve orphans in order to collect $1200 in government stipends.
